convergenceRule: early-stop on 3 consecutive iters with zero new P0+P1

perIterAngle.1: regression-risk — schema.ts tx-wrap claim vs actual diff (no BEGIN/COMMIT), P0-C cap-removal score-distribution side-effects, public export of vecTableNameForDim, deleted local tableNameForDim callers
perIterAngle.2: correctness-completeness — 3 P0 fixes: are they semantically complete? Commit msg says 'INSERT-OR-IGNORE binds caller args + tx wrap' but diff shows no transaction wrapping; P0-C cap raise to 1.0 — clamp upstream verified?; P0-B docType normalization tweak — any other docType string-equality compares that share the same root cause?
perIterAngle.3: P1-Group-1 completeness — were ALL input-validation gaps closed across config.py / handlers / reindex? Are there sibling files (embedder-status.ts, adapter.ts, registered_embedders.py) with similar gaps still open? parseBoundedEnv helper coverage check
perIterAngle.4: P1-Group-3 observability — are the audit/error/rescue-telemetry logs structured + greppable? Any sensitive data leaked (embedder names with secrets, db paths, env values)? Are log levels appropriate (debug for telemetry, info for swap, error for failure)?
perIterAngle.5: P1-Group-4 architecture — registry.ts llama-cpp gate removal: any callers depending on the removed throw? Manifest-name parity broken? tableNameForDim dedupe in reindex.ts → vecTableNameForDim re-export from schema.ts: any orphan callers in other modules?
perIterAngle.6: security — parseBoundedEnv env-var validation: any injection vectors via integer overflow / NaN / Infinity? logger calls: any format-string risks (printf-style injection)? Python _resolve_device allowlist: complete vs PyTorch backend names? config.py warnings: log injection via env values?
perIterAngle.7: adversarial-residual — explicitly hunt for things iter 1-6 missed; if all clear, look for new P0/P1 in same files; check the 7 new vitest cases for false-positive coverage (do they actually exercise the bug, or just exercise the happy path?)

knownContext.commitMsgVsCodeDelta: commit msg claims 'tx wrap' for P0-A but diff shows only binding correction — no BEGIN/COMMIT statements added; this gap is the seed observation for iter 2
